message:
perf(tools): replace eager Vec allocation with lazy memchr iteration in truncation

Optimize truncate_head() and truncate_tail() to avoid allocating a
Vec<&str> of all line slices when processing large file content:

truncate_head:
- Use memchr::memchr_iter to count total lines (newline counting) instead
  of split().collect() — avoids ~800KB pointer-array allocation for a
  100K-line file
- Use memchr::memchr to find first newline for first-line-length check
  without collecting all lines
- Iterate via split('\n') lazily instead of indexing into a collected Vec

truncate_tail:
- Use rsplit('\n') for lazy reverse iteration instead of collecting into
  Vec then iterating in reverse
- Introduce TailLine<'a> enum to borrow &str slices from the input buffer
  instead of cloning each line into a String, only allocating for partial
  last lines
- Pre-allocate final output String with known byte_count capacity
- Use memchr_iter for total line count

Adds memchr 2.7 dependency (zero-allocation, SIMD-accelerated byte
searching).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
